skill.name: encori-api
skill.description: Access ENCORI (StarBase) database for miRNA-target, RNA-RNA, and other regulatory data. Invoke when user asks to search ENCORI or retrieve regulatory interactions.
skill.summary: Programmatic query tool for the ENCORI (StarBase) RNA interaction database supporting miRNA-target, RNA-RNA, ceRNA, and RBP data retrieval.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: This skill provides legitimate access to the ENCORI (StarBase) bioinformatics database API. Static analysis flagged 65 potential issues; all high-severity findings (weak cryptographic algorithm, system reconnaissance) and medium-severity findings (shell backtick execution) were evaluated as false positives upon context review. The remaining true positive findings (HTTP client library, hardcoded URL) reflect the skill's documented function as an API client for rnasysu.com. No malicious intent, data exfiltration, credential theft, or prompt injection detected. The skill is safe to publish with a medium risk level due to inherent network and external command execution factors.

medium_findings.title: External API Network Requests
medium_findings.description: The encori_client.py script makes HTTP GET requests to the ENCORI (StarBase) database API at rnasysu.com. This is the documented core function of the skill and is not malicious. Network access is required for database queries and is expected behavior.

medium_findings.title: External Python Script Execution
medium_findings.description: The SKILL.md instructs execution of Python scripts (encori_client.py, validate_skill.py) via shell commands. These are documented, validated scripts that query the ENCORI database. No arbitrary command injection vector exists as arguments are parsed via argparse.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Pattern (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at multiple locations. All are false positives: encori-api_audit_result_v2.json is a data file with no crypto code, scripts/encori_client.py lines 121-122 are argparse definitions, scripts/validate_skill.py line 12 is an argparse description, and SKILL.md lines are markdown documentation text. No actual cryptographic operations exist in any file.

low_findings.title: Shell Backtick Commands in Markdown (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 36 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in SKILL.md. All instances are markdown code blocks (```bash ... ```) containing example usage commands for documentation purposes. None are active/executable backtick commands embedded in the file. These are safe documentation examples.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Pattern (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'system reconnaissance' at encori-api_audit_result_v2.json lines 23, 190, 260 and SKILL.md line 48. All instances are legitimate documentation text describing audit results and output discipline practices. No actual reconnaissance behavior exists.

dangerous_patterns.title: Process Termination on Error
dangerous_patterns.description: encori_client.py calls sys.exit(1) on API request failure. If invoked from within an AI runtime, this could terminate the calling process. Consider raising an exception instead to allow graceful error handling.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unsantitized Raw Output Printing
dangerous_patterns.description: encori_client.py prints raw API response text directly to stdout without content validation or sanitization. A compromised ENCORI API endpoint could inject unexpected content into the output stream.

content.user_title: Query ENCORI Database for RNA and miRNA Interactions
content.value_statement: Bioinformatics researchers need a programmatic way to access the ENCORI (StarBase) database for miRNA-target, RNA-RNA, and RBP interaction data. This skill provides a command-line interface to query multiple ENCORI endpoints and retrieve structured interaction evidence.
actual_capabilities: Query miRNA-target interactions supported by Ago CLIP-seq experimental data from ENCORI
Retrieve RNA-RNA and ceRNA interaction networks for specified genes and miRNA families
Search RNA-binding protein (RBP) targets with CLIP-seq experimental support data
Retrieve RBP-disease associations including somatic mutation data for specified tissues
Query binding motifs of RNA-binding proteins and CLIP-seq binding site data
Search miRNA cleavage events from degradome-seq experiments for target genes
limitations: Requires internet access to connect to the ENCORI database API at rnasysu.com
Results depend on the completeness and accuracy of the ENCORI (StarBase) database version
Does not perform statistical analysis or biological interpretation of retrieved results
Data is limited to what is available in the ENCORI database and may not include unpublished studies

use_cases.title: miRNA-Target Interaction Search
use_cases.description: Search for miRNA binding targets of a specific gene using CLIP-seq supported interaction data from ENCORI.

use_cases.title: RNA-RNA Network Analysis
use_cases.description: Retrieve non-coding RNA interaction networks for a target gene to understand regulatory relationships.

use_cases.title: RBP-Disease Association Mining
use_cases.description: Find RNA-binding proteins and their target genes associated with specific diseases and tissues.

prompt_templates.title: Basic miRNA Target Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Search ENCORI for miRNA targets of the gene TP53 and return interaction data.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user querying a basic miRNA-target interaction

prompt_templates.title: RBP Binding Site Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Retrieve all RNA-binding proteins that bind to TP53 in HeLa cells from ENCORI and list the results.
prompt_templates.scenario: RNA biologist studying protein-RNA interactions in a specific cell line

prompt_templates.title: ceRNA Network Discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Get ceRNA networks for the miR-10-5p family from ENCORI with a p-value threshold of 0.05.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced researcher exploring competing endogenous RNA networks

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Endpoint Data Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: First query ENCORI for miRNA targets of BRCA1, then retrieve the RBP-disease associations for BRCA1 in breast tissue.
prompt_templates.scenario: Power user running sequential queries across different ENCORI data modules

output_examples.input: Search ENCORI for miRNA targets of PDCD4 in HeLa cells.
output_examples.output: A table of miRNA-PDCD4 interactions showing miRNA ID, target site location, CLIP-seq experiment count, and predicted program support from the ENCORI database.

output_examples.input: Find RBP-disease associations for MYC in breast carcinoma from ENCORI.
output_examples.output: A list of RNA-binding proteins that interact with MYC alongside associated somatic mutations in breast carcinoma, organized by assembly version, tissue type, and disease type.

output_examples.input: Get RNA-RNA interaction networks for TP53 from ENCORI.
output_examples.output: A structured list of non-coding RNAs that interact with TP53 mRNA, showing interaction counts, supporting experiment numbers, and cell type information.

best_practices: Always specify the assembly version (hg38 or mm10) to ensure results match your reference genome of interest
Use specific gene names instead of broad queries to reduce result size and improve data relevance
Validate network connectivity and input parameters before running queries to avoid timeout errors
anti_patterns: Querying all data without filters may return excessively large result sets that are difficult to process
Using this skill for direct clinical decisions without understanding database limitations and coverage gaps
Running multiple parallel queries without checking if the ENCORI API rate-limits incoming requests

faq.question: What is ENCORI?
faq.answer: ENCORI (The Encyclopedia of RNA Interactomes), formerly known as StarBase, is a database of RNA interactions including miRNA-target, RNA-RNA, and RBP-target data supported by CLIP-seq experiments.

faq.question: What data types does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports miRNA-target, degradome, RNA-RNA, ceRNA, RBP-target, RBP-disease, RBP motif scan, and binding site queries from the ENCORI database.

faq.question: Is an internet connection required?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill requires internet access to connect to the ENCORI database API at rnasysu.com for data retrieval.

faq.question: What genome assemblies are supported?
faq.answer: The default assembly is hg38 (human). Mouse genome mm10 is also supported by the ENCORI database.

faq.question: Can I export results to a file?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill returns structured text data (TSV or CSV-like format) that can be saved to a result file for further analysis.

faq.question: What are the limitations of this data?
faq.answer: Results depend on the completeness of the ENCORI database. The data may not include the most recent unpublished studies and covers only interactions archived in the database.
